title: Rail construction and maintenance operatives
description: Perform engineering duties in planning, designing, and overseeing construction and maintenance of building structures and facilities, such as roads, railroads, airports, bridges, harbors, channels, dams, irrigation projects, pipelines, power plants, and water and sewage systems. Develop plans for surface transportation projects, according to established engineering standards and state or federal construction policy. Prepare designs, specifications, or estimates for transportation facilities. Plan modifications of existing streets, highways, or freeways to improve traffic flow. Design or oversee projects involving provision of potable water, disposal of wastewater and sewage, or prevention of flood-related damage. Prepare environmental documentation for water resources, regulatory program compliance, data management and analysis, and field work. Perform hydraulic modeling and pipeline design. (Based on 18 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Review schedules, way bills, and shipping records to obtain cargo loading and unloading information and to plan work.
Confer with engineers regarding train routes, timetables, and cargoes, and discuss alternative routes due to rail defects or obstructions.
Inspect freight cars for compliance with sealing procedures, document time and service records, and supervise inspection and maintenance activities.
Document and prepare reports of accidents, unscheduled stops, and delays, while keeping records of train operations and conditions.
Signal engineers to initiate or stop train runs, change speed, and ensure safe coupling or uncoupling of train cars using communication methods.
Inspect locomotives, tracks, cars, and systems for defects, and conduct tests to evaluate safety and functioning of equipment.
Design and oversee transportation or hydraulic system projects, preparing plans, specifications, budgets, and ensuring compliance with standards.
Conduct feasibility studies and environmental impact assessments for transportation projects, offering technical advice and analyses.
Develop and implement maintenance plans for transportation systems and components, while supervising construction and repair activities.
Clean and repair drainage systems and culverts, while maintaining roadways and inspecting road signage and safety equipment.
Engage in track maintenance activities including welding, cutting, and aligning tracks, and adjusting machinery for optimal functioning.
Operate and maintain heavy machinery for construction tasks including asphalt repair, debris removal, and snow clearing.
Analyze and recommend methodologies for waste treatment, including designing treatment plants and disposal methods for efficiency.
Prepare and present public reports on construction projects and environmental impact statements, and participate in stakeholder meetings.
Perform hydrological analyses and develop plans for water resource management, ensuring compliance with environmental standards and regulations.
Patrol assigned track sections to locate and report damaged or broken track.
Clean, grade, or level ballast on railroad tracks and drive vehicles like graders to distribute gravel or ballast between rails.
Operate spike pullers to pull old spikes from ties and use tie-adzing machines to prepare ties for rail insertion.
Drive machines to lay tracks over sections to be constructed, repaired, or maintained, ensuring proper alignment and leveling.
Repair or adjust track switches using wrenches and replacement parts, and inspect project sites to evaluate contractor work for compliance.
Produce or update overlay maps that show geographical information necessary for construction projects.
Conduct field surveys to locate new sites for projects and analyze existing site details.
Inspect bridges, highways, and other infrastructure to ensure adherence to safety standards and compliance with building codes.
Prepare detailed drawings and specifications for projects, ensuring conformance to dimensions, materials, and regulatory standards.
Report maintenance problems at project sites to supervisors and negotiate necessary changes to resolve issues.
Collect and analyze data related to energy usage and energy efficiency measures in building or infrastructure projects.
Confer with supervisors to review project details such as plan preparation and field conditions, ensuring project compliance.
Calculate dimensions, angles, or other relevant information necessary for accurate mapping and construction planning.
Plan work assignments and equipment allocations to meet transportation, operations, or production goals.
Inspect or test materials, stock, vehicles, equipment, or facilities to ensure that they are safe, free of defects, and consistent with specifications.
Monitor field work to ensure proper performance and use of materials.
Maintain or verify records of time, materials, expenditures, or crew activities.
Prepare, compile, and submit reports on work activities, operations, production, or work-related accidents.
Confer with customers, supervisors, contractors, or other personnel to exchange information or to resolve problems.
Enforce safety rules and regulations during operational activities.
Recommend and implement measures to improve worker motivation, equipment performance, work methods, or customer services.
Drive vehicles or operate machines or equipment to complete work assignments or assist workers.
Direct workers in transportation or related services, such as loading or unloading of materials.
Requisition needed personnel, supplies, equipment, parts, or repair services.
Inspect work areas or operating equipment to ensure conformance to established standards.
